prompt_text: Compare Sentry and Datadog for catch and fix production errors before users notice
response:
# Sentry vs Datadog for Production Error Monitoring

## Quick Comparison

| Factor | Sentry | Datadog |
|--------|--------|---------|
| **Primary Focus** | Error tracking & debugging | Full observability platform |
| **Setup Speed** | ⚡ Very fast (minutes) | ⏱️ Moderate (days) |
| **Price** | 💰 Lower (error-focused) | 💸 Higher (all-in) |
| **Learning Curve** | 📊 Gentler | 📈 Steeper |

---

## Sentry: Specialized Error Tracker

### Strengths
✅ **Blazing fast implementation** – Capture errors in minutes  
✅ **Deep debugging** – Source maps, breadcrumbs, user context  
✅ **Developer-friendly** – Built for engineers, by engineers  
✅ **Lower cost** – Pay only for errors you want to track  
✅ **Best-in-class error grouping** – Automatically clusters similar issues  

### Weaknesses
❌ Limited metrics/performance monitoring  
❌ No infrastructure visibility  
❌ Less suited for DevOps/platform teams  

### Best For
- Startups & small teams wanting quick error detection
- Frontend-heavy applications
- Teams that want a single-purpose tool

---

## Datadog: All-in-One Platform

### Strengths
✅ **Complete observability** – Errors + metrics + logs + APM + infrastructure  
✅ **Single pane of glass** – Correlate errors with system behavior  
✅ **Enterprise features** – Advanced RBAC, compliance, SLOs  
✅ **Powerful alerting** – Cross-domain automation  
✅ **Infrastructure monitoring** – Servers, containers, cloud-native stacks  

### Weaknesses
❌ **Expensive** – Costs add up (logs, metrics, APM stacks)  
❌ **Steeper learning curve** – More complex configuration  
❌ **Overkill for small teams** – Heavy tool for simple use cases  

### Best For
- Large enterprises with complex architectures
- Teams needing full-stack observability
- Organizations willing to invest in unified tooling

---

## Decision Matrix

```
Choose SENTRY if you need:
├─ Fast time-to-value
├─ Focused error catching
├─ Lower budget
└─ Quick team onboarding

Choose DATADOG if you need:
├─ Full observability (logs, metrics, traces, errors)
├─ Infrastructure monitoring
├─ Advanced automation/SLOs
└─ Larger budget + complex infrastructure
```

---

## Practical Recommendation

**Start with Sentry**, add Datadog later if you need:
- Infrastructure monitoring
- Log aggregation
- Complex correlation analysis
- Enterprise governance

This approach lets you catch errors quickly without over-engineering from day one.
